zh.lifeSummary: Sabina Vollmer於1739年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加宾夕法尼亚州蘭卡斯特貝瑟爾 堂史普。她於1760年11月25日與Johann Friederich Kieffer在美國賓夕法尼亞州勒班農貝瑟爾 堂史普結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在美國賓夕法尼亞州亞當斯利特萊斯托。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sabina Vollmer于1739年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加宾夕法尼亚州蘭卡斯特貝瑟爾 堂史普。她于1760年11月25日与Johann Friederich Kieffer在美国宾夕法尼亚州勒班農貝瑟爾 堂史普结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世在美国宾夕法尼亚州亞當斯利特萊斯托。

nameMeanings.name: Vollmer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements folk ‘people’ + meri, mari ‘famous’. This surname is also found in France (mainly Alsace). Compare Follmer , Fullmer , and Volmer .

variants.name: Follmer
meanings: Americanized form of German   Vollmer  . Compare   Folmer   and   Fullmer  .

variants.name: Volkmer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erik, Hans, Reiner.

variants.name: Volmer
meanings: Dutch: from the personal name Volmer, a cognate of German Vollmer , or an adaptation of the German surname. Compare Folmer .

variants.name: Volz
meanings: German (also Völz): from a pet form of the personal name Vol(k)mar or Volkmer (see Vollmer ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Voelz and Voltz , and also Faltz and Folz .

nameMeanings.name: Sabina
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: name meaning ‘Sabine woman’, used occasionally in England since the 14th century. The Sabines were an ancient Italic race whose territory was early taken over by the Romans. According to tradition, the Romans made a raid on the Sabines and carried off a number of their women, but when the Sabines came for revenge the women succeeded in making peace between the two groups. The name Sabina was borne by three minor early Christian saints, in particular a Roman maiden martyred in about 127 .

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.
The Declaration of Independence was signed in Philadelphia on July 4, 1776. The liberty bell was first rung here to Celebrate this important document.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.
Historical Boundaries 1810: Adams, Pennsylvania, United States

historicEvents.title: The Second Great Awakening
historicEvents.description: Being a second spiritual and religious awakening, like the First Great Awakening, many Churches began to spring up from other denominations. Many people began to rapidly join the Baptist and Methodist congregations. Many converts to these religions believed that the Awakening was the precursor of a new millennial age.

historicEvents.title: The Oregon Trail
historicEvents.description: Many people started their 2,170-mile West trek to settle the land found by Louis and Clark. They used large-wheeled wagons to pack most of their belongings and were guided by trails that were made by the previous trappers and traders who walked the area. Over time the trail needed annual improvements to make the trip faster and safer. Most of Interstate 80 and 84 cover most of the ground that was the original trail.

en.lifeSummary: Sabina Vollmer was born in 1739, in Bethel Township, Lancaster, Pennsylvania, British Colonial America. She married Johann Friederich Kieffer on 25 November 1760, in Bethel Township, Lebanon, Pennsylvania, United States. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 1 daughter. She died in Littlestown, Adams, Pennsylvania, United States.

Elle est décédée à Littlestown, Adams, Pennsylvanie, États-Unis.

ja.lifeSummary: Sabina Vollmerは1739年にイギリス植民地アメリカペンシルベニア州ランカスターベスエル トウンシュイップで生まれた。1760年11月25日にアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州レバノンベスエル トウンシュイップでJohann Friederich Kiefferと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女はアメリカ合衆国ペンシルベニア州アダムズリットレストウンで死去した。

ko.Bethel Twp,Lancaster,Pa: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 펜실베이니아 주 랭커스터 베테 타운시프
ko.Bethel, Lebanon, Pennsylvania, United St: 미국 펜실베이니아 주 레바넌 베테 타운시프
ko.lifeSummary: Sabina Vollmer(은)는 1739년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 펜실베이니아 주 랭커스터 베테 타운시프에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1760년 11월 25일 미국 펜실베이니아 주 레바넌 베테 타운시프에서 Johann Friederich Kieffer(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 미국 펜실베이니아 주 애댐스 리틀레스타운에서 사망했다.

ru.Bethel, Lebanon, Pennsylvania, United St: бетел тоуншип, лебанон, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты
ru.lifeSummary: Sabina Vollmer родилась в 1739 г. в местности бетел тоуншип, ланкастир, Пенсильвания, бритиш колоняль амирика. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Johann Friederich Kieffer 25 ноября 1760 г. в местности бетел тоуншип, лебанон, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в местности литлестоун, адамс, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Sabina Vollmer nació en 1739, en Bethel Township, Lancaster, Pensilvania, British Colonial America. Se casó con Johann Friederich Kieffer el 25 de noviembre de 1760, en Bethel Township, Lebanon, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en Littlestown, Adams, Pensilvania, Estados Unidos.
